recipe_followed_in_code.evidence: dds <- DESeqDataSetFromMatrix(countData = counts_matrix, colData = metadata_df, design = ~ cell_type)
recipe_followed_in_code.why: Uses DESeq2 API correctly but skips pseudobulk aggregation by donor — required for single-cell input by the deseq2-r recipe; each column is a cell, not a biological replicate.

method_validity.evidence: dds <- DESeqDataSetFromMatrix(countData = counts_matrix, colData = metadata_df, design = ~ cell_type)
method_validity.why: DESeq2 run directly on individual cells as columns with design ~ cell_type is pseudoreplication; cells within a donor are not independent replicates and this inflates degrees of freedom severely.

counts <- as.matrix(counts)

metadata <- metadata[colnames(counts), , drop = FALSE]
